curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Recognise surds as irrational roots, simplify surds by factorising into square factors, and manipulate surds by adding/subtracting like surds and multiplying using √a·√b = √(ab), applying clear rules and communication strategies to stay accurate. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

verse_ality.driving_question: How can we work confidently with irrational square roots using reliable rules?

knowing_world.focus: Surds are irrational roots that cannot be simplified to whole or fractional numbers, but they can be manipulated using consistent rules: simplify by extracting square factors, combine only like surds when adding/subtracting, and multiply using √a·√b = √(ab).
key_concepts: Surd as an irrational square root (e.g., √2, √3, √5)
Simplifying surds by factorising into square factors
Like surds (same root part) combine like algebra terms
Treat surds like algebraic terms unless simplified first
activities: Work out √8 + √18 (simplify first, then combine if like).

human_story.focus: Surds emerged from geometry needs like diagonals and lengths that cannot be written exactly as fractions; discoveries such as √2 reshaped ancient mathematical thinking about number. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

expressive_self.focus: Students communicate surd rules creatively to strengthen memory and build confidence explaining irrational numbers to others.
activities: Design a visual comparing rational roots and irrational surds.
Write a poem starting: “Square roots that never end…”
Make a short video explaining surds to a friend who struggles with fractions.

designed_world.focus: Use an interactive practice tool to practise simplifying, adding/subtracting, and multiplying surds across different challenge levels with feedback and repeat attempts.
tools: Canva embed: Surd practice tool (levels for simplifying, adding/subtracting, multiplying; Check and Try Another)
activities: Click “Check” for feedback and “Try Another” for more practice.

regulation_supports: Use string or sticks to form right-angled triangles. Measure the hypotenuse and estimate its square root; compare estimates with others.

pathways.name: Final Task - Surds Mini Guide / Poster
pathways.focus: Create a short guide or poster explaining how to simplify, add/subtract, and multiply surds, with clear examples and a real-world usefulness note.
products: A short guide or poster on manipulating surds
At least one example each of simplifying, adding/subtracting, and multiplying surds
Colour-coding or visual cues showing which parts combine and why
A short explanation of how surds are useful beyond school maths
assessment_criteria: Conceptual Understanding: Rules are explained correctly (simplify first; only like surds combine; √a·√b = √(ab))
Fluency & Accuracy: Examples are correct and simplified fully
Communication: Colour-coding/structure makes steps easy to follow
Application & Reflection: Includes a meaningful note on why surds matter beyond school

required: Know square numbers and factor pairs
Understand square roots as the inverse of squaring
Basic algebra idea of combining like terms

builds_toward: Exact form answers in Pythagoras and trigonometry
Quadratic surds and simplifying expressions in algebra
